name: sidguesser
title: sidguesser

commands.name: sidguess
commands.raw_help:
root@kali:~# sidguess -h
 
 SIDGuesser v1.0.5 by patrik@cqure.net
 -------------------------------------
 sidguess -i <ip> -d <dictionary> [options]
 
 options:
   -p <portnr> Use specific port (default 1521)
   -r <report> Report to file
   -m <mode>   findfirst OR findall(default)

examples.command:
sidguess -i 192.168.1.205 -d /usr/share/wordlists/metasploit/unix_users.txt

SIDGuesser v1.0.5 by patrik@cqure.net
-------------------------------------

Starting Dictionary Attack (<space> for stats, Q for quit) ...

binary_metadata.file_type: ELF 64-bit LSB pie executable, x86-64, version 1 (SYSV), dynamically linked, interpreter /lib64/ld-linux-x86-64.so.2, BuildID[sha1]=1192b6a3f32521a4fd456a62e1f91729994f6b95, for GNU/Linux 3.2.0, stripped

apt_metadata.description:
Guesses sids against an Oracle database
Guesses sids/instances against an Oracle database according
to a predefined dictionary file. The speed is slow (80-100
guesses per second) but it does the job.

use_cases: When you need Kali Linux, use sidguess because ### sidguesser.
When chaining Kali Linux tools, pair sidguess with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `sidguess --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.

documentation.when_to_use: Use during the 'any' phase against surfaces [web] in the Kali Linux category. Fits when the engagement needs: ### sidguesser.
usage_sections: Install with: sudo apt install sidguesser
